title: cyperus pohlii subsp. bahiensis
intro: Cyperus pohlii subsp. bahiensis, also known as the Bahian papyrus sedge, is a subspecies of the Cyperus pohlii plant, belonging to the family Cyperaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Cyperus pohlii subsp. bahiensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable resource for treating various health conditions. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the form of essential oils and extracts for various therapeutic applications.
